articles.title: CRYPTO MARKET SLIPS INTO ‘EXTREME FEAR,' BTC DROPS BELOW $100,000
articles.summary: The crypto Fear & Greed Index plunged to 10, marking extreme fear levels unseen since late February, as Bitcoin dropped over 5% in a week to trade below $96,000 after failing twice this month to hold the $100,000 level. The selloff stems from long-term holder profit-taking, institutional outflows, leveraged liquidations, and macro uncertainty as Federal Reserve rate cut odds fell to 50% while the recent government shutdown delayed key economic data releases. Order-book depth across major centralized exchanges remains structurally lower since October's crash, creating low liquidity conditions that risk amplified price swings.

articles.title: HARVARD TRIPLES BITCOIN ETF HOLDINGS TO $443M
articles.summary: Harvard University's $57 billion endowment tripled its spot bitcoin ETF exposure in Q3, holding 6.8 million shares of BlackRock's IBIT, up 257% from June, making it Harvard's largest declared US holding ahead of Microsoft and Amazon. Valued at $442.8 million at the time, now around $364 million after IBIT's price decline, the position equals roughly 0.6% of the endowment.

articles.title: AERO: ONE TOKEN. ONE ENGINE. ONE LIQUIDITY LAYER
articles.summary: Aerodrome and Velodrome are merging into Aero with a single AERO token launching Q2 2026 on Base as the operational hub, distributing 94.5% to AERO holders and 5.5% to VELO holders based on their $260 million versus $15 million in trailing 52-week revenue. The MetaDEX03 architecture expands revenue capture beyond swap fees to include front-end routing, internal MEV auctions, cross-chain bridging, and liquidity automation targeting a $7 billion market, with all value flowing to AERO token holders. Aero deploys across Ethereum mainnet, Arc, and Syndicate using hub-and-spoke architecture, while the new Momentum Fund replaces existing programs to direct rewards, execute rule-based buybacks and burns, and compensate high-impact contributors with veAERO tokens.

articles.title: MOONPAY LAUNCHES ENTERPRISE STABLECOIN ISSUANCE PLATFORM
articles.summary: MoonPay has launched a new enterprise-grade stablecoin issuance business powered by Iron and M0, enabling partners to create app-specific, interoperable stablecoins issued directly from the US and distributed globally through MoonPay's buy, sell, swap, deposit, and checkout network.

articles.title: UNPACKING THE DAI STACK
articles.summary: The deAI stack comprises three interoperable layers: x402 from Coinbase and Cloudflare for agent payments via stablecoins at the application layer, ERC 8004 as an onchain discovery registry mapping agent IDs to capabilities using NFT-based AgentCards, and Google's A2A protocol for handling transport-layer communication through JSON-RPC over HTTPS. These standards mirror the traditional internet stack, where TCP/IP handles transport, DNS manages discovery, and HTTP enables applications, but extends functionality to allow agents to transact and coordinate with onchain resources. The architecture enables complete workflows like purchasing a refrigerator, where ERC 8004 discovers the merchant agent, A2A facilitates communication over HTTP endpoints, and X402 processes stablecoin payments and authorization.

articles.title: UAE FINANCIAL SERVICES CONSOLIDATION CONTROVERSY
articles.summary: The UAE's Federal-Decree Law No. 6 of 2025 criminalizes unlicensed financial activities with imprisonment and fines up to AED 500 million ($136 million), while Article 62's "facilitation" language extends regulatory reach to technology providers offering infrastructure that enables financial services. Critics argue the law effectively bans self-custodial Bitcoin wallets, blockchain explorers, and similar tools for UAE citizens without Central Bank licensing, as it captures entities "directly or indirectly" facilitating licensed activities even when not offering underlying financial products.

articles.title: ETH'S TIME WILL COME IN EARLY 2026
articles.summary: While ETH's chart looks weak now, it's entering a slow consolidation near key ETH/BTC support that typically precedes stronger cycles, with the real move likely between late Q4 2025 and Q1 2026. The end of quantitative tightening in December and a potential shift toward QE could release liquidity back into markets, favoring risk assets like ETH and altcoins once the Reverse Repo facility empties and the Fed stops absorbing capital.

articles.title: DEFI YIELD HAS EVOLVED
articles.summary: DeFi yield protocols have evolved from 2020's degen farming to institutional-grade structured income, led by Aave v4's $40B cross-chain liquidity layer with dynamic risk pricing and Morpho's $11B efficiency layer that supercharges Aave and Compound rates. Pendle dominates yield tokenization at $6B TVL with its new Boros product packaging perpetual funding rates into tradable assets, while Solstice builds Solana's institutional yield infrastructure at $270M TVL, offering fixed, variable, and boosted delta-neutral returns. Emerging players include Katana's yield-first L2 delivering 8-41% APYs on $249M TVL and Turtle's campaign-driven router reaching 40.8% APY in its Yearn USD vault across $600M in deposits.

articles.title: AERODROME EXPANDS BEYOND BASE WITH ETHEREUM INTEGRATION
articles.summary: Aerodrome's move from Base to Ethereum isn't a loss for Base but proof of its success.

articles.title: LINK SUPPLY DRIES UP AS INSTITUTIONS ACCUMULATE
articles.summary: This ultimately suggests market makers are pushing retail out while larger players continue to accumulate through the downturn.

articles.title: ETHEREUM FLIPS BITCOIN IN TREASURY HOLDINGS
articles.summary: Data from Artemis shows Ethereum now leads Bitcoin in the percentage of total supply held by digital asset treasuries, with ETH at 0.043% compared to BTC's 0.036%.

articles.title: AERODROME BRIBE REVENUE MISCONCEPTIONS DEBUNKED
articles.summary: Bribes function like a “cost” for protocols to boost liquidity and emissions, similar to secondary revenue streams in traditional businesses.
